seo.title: 10 Best Hotels in Chanthaburi 2026 — Budget to Boutique
seo.metaDescription: Find the best hotel in Chanthaburi for your budget. Our 2026 guide covers 10 top-rated properties from budget guesthouses to riverside boutiques — with real prices, locations and honest tips.

intro: Chanthaburi's hotel scene is small but surprisingly varied. As a city that caters primarily to Thai domestic tourists and gem traders rather than foreign visitors, you will find excellent value across all categories. Budget guesthouses near the Old Town start at 400 THB, comfortable mid-range hotels cluster around the city center for 800–2,000 THB, and a handful of atmospheric boutique properties offer unique heritage experiences. Below are 10 properties we recommend across budget, mid-range and boutique/luxury categories, with real 2026 price ranges.

hotels.name: K.P. Grand Hotel
hotels.description: A reliable budget hotel in the heart of Chanthaburi within walking distance of the gem market and Waterfront Community. Rooms are simple but clean with air-conditioning, hot water and free WiFi. The location is excellent for exploring the Old Town on foot, and there are multiple street food stalls and restaurants within a 2-minute walk.
highlights: Walking distance to Gem Street and Waterfront Community
Clean rooms with reliable air-conditioning and hot water
Free WiFi throughout the property
On-site parking for guests with rental cars
Multiple restaurants and convenience stores nearby

hotels.name: Baan Luang Rajamaitri Historic Inn
hotels.description: A charming guesthouse in a restored heritage shophouse right on the Chanthaburi Waterfront. The building itself is part of the heritage experience — wooden floors, period furniture and a traditional Thai breakfast included. Rooms are modest in size but full of character, and you wake up in the most atmospheric quarter of the city.
highlights: Located inside the Waterfront Community heritage quarter
Restored heritage shophouse with period character
Traditional Thai breakfast included
Walking distance to Cathedral and Gem Street
Friendly English-speaking owner with local knowledge

hotels.name: Maneechan Resort
hotels.description: A clean, no-frills budget resort on the southern edge of the city center with spacious rooms, a small garden and on-site parking. Popular with Thai families and gem traders during the weekend market. Rooms are larger than typical budget options, and the friendly staff can help arrange motorcycle rental and orchard tours.
highlights: Spacious rooms at very low prices
On-site parking — ideal for self-drive visitors
Small garden and outdoor seating area
Staff can arrange local tours and transport
15-minute walk to Gem Street and Old Town

hotels.name: Tamajun Hotel
hotels.description: The most popular mid-range hotel in Chanthaburi, Tamajun offers modern, well-furnished rooms with strong air-conditioning, rain showers and comfortable beds. The on-site restaurant serves good Thai food, and the hotel is within easy reach of all city center attractions. Staff are helpful with local recommendations and tour arrangements.
highlights: Modern rooms with rain showers and quality bedding
On-site restaurant with Thai and international options
Central location near Gem Street and Waterfront
Free WiFi and on-site parking
Helpful staff with local tour recommendations

hotels.name: Napalai Hotel
hotels.description: A well-maintained mid-range hotel close to the main commercial district with comfortable rooms, a small fitness area and a pleasant lobby café. Napalai is a solid choice for visitors who want clean, reliable accommodation without paying boutique prices. The hotel caters to both Thai and international guests.
highlights: Comfortable rooms with modern amenities
Lobby café serving breakfast and light meals
Small fitness area for guests
Close to the main commercial district and restaurants
Good value for the quality offered

hotels.name: Baan Rim Nam Resort
hotels.description: A peaceful riverside resort set among tropical gardens on the outskirts of Chanthaburi. Bungalow-style rooms face the river, offering a tranquil escape from the city center. The resort has a small swimming pool, a riverside restaurant serving fresh seafood and lush gardens where tropical birds visit in the morning.
highlights: Riverside bungalows surrounded by tropical gardens
Small swimming pool and riverside dining area
Restaurant serving fresh local seafood
Peaceful setting away from city noise
Free parking and easy access to national parks

hotels.name: Chill D River Boutique Hotel
hotels.description: A contemporary boutique-style hotel near the river with stylish rooms, a café and a rooftop terrace. Chill D River blends modern design with Chanthaburi's riverside character. It is one of the newer properties in the city, attracting younger Thai travellers and the occasional foreign visitor looking for comfort with style.
highlights: Contemporary design with riverside character
Rooftop terrace with city and river views
On-site café serving specialty coffee and Thai food
Walking distance to Waterfront Community
Modern bathrooms with quality fixtures

hotels.name: Chanthaburi Riverside Hotel
hotels.description: The closest thing to a full-service luxury hotel in Chanthaburi, this riverside property offers spacious rooms with river views, a swimming pool, spa, fitness center and multiple restaurants. It is the preferred choice for visiting gem dealers and affluent Thai tourists. The riverside location and full amenities set make it a comfortable base for exploring the province.
highlights: Swimming pool with riverside views
Full-service spa and fitness center
Multiple restaurants including riverside dining
Spacious rooms with river-view balconies
Conference facilities and business center

hotels.name: The Wooden House Chanthaburi
hotels.description: An intimate heritage boutique hotel housed in a beautifully restored traditional wooden building near the Old Town. Each room is individually decorated with antique furniture and local art. The property captures the essence of old Chanthaburi, with wooden verandas, a quiet garden courtyard and personalized service from the owner-operators who share local stories and recommendations.
highlights: Restored traditional wooden architecture with antique furnishings
Individually decorated rooms with local art
Quiet garden courtyard
Personalized service from owner-operators
Walking distance to all Old Town attractions

hotels.name: Chan Ocha Resort
hotels.description: A nature-focused resort set among tropical gardens on the outskirts of Chanthaburi, convenient for visiting Namtok Phlio and Khao Kitchakut national parks. The resort features private villas with outdoor bathtubs, a large swimming pool, a spa and a restaurant specializing in local cuisine with orchard-fresh fruit. It is the most premium accommodation option in the Chanthaburi area.
highlights: Private villas with outdoor bathtubs
Large swimming pool set in tropical gardens
On-site spa and wellness center
Restaurant featuring local cuisine with orchard-fresh fruit
Convenient base for national park visits

bookingTips: Book ahead during the Fruit Festival (May–June) and King Taksin Festival (late December) — accommodation fills quickly with Thai domestic tourists.
Weekend prices can be 20–30% higher than weekday rates due to the Friday/Saturday gem market drawing traders from Bangkok.
Most Chanthaburi hotels include free parking — useful since you will likely need a car or motorcycle for national parks and beaches.
Agoda and Booking.com have the best selection for Chanthaburi; some smaller guesthouses are only on Agoda or accept walk-ins.
Consider staying in the Old Town/Waterfront area if you want to explore on foot; stay on the outskirts for easier access to national parks.
Air-conditioning quality varies at budget hotels — check recent reviews mentioning A/C before booking in the hot months.
Chanthaburi is excellent value overall — you can stay in the city's best hotel for what you would pay for a mid-range room in Bangkok.
